plan: Retrieve account details for user `mohamed_silva_9265` to locate gift cards and certificates.
Sum all gift card balances and report the total.
Sum all certificate balances and report the total.
Look up the most recent reservation to get the current itinerary (origin, destination, dates, cabin class, passengers).
Search for the cheapest available business class round trip matching those exact dates (any number of stops).
Determine whether the current reservation can be changed (check if it is basic economy or another changeable fare).
If changeable: calculate the cost to change to the cheapest business option, then compute how much master card would be charged after applying certificates first, then gift cards.
If not changeable (e.g., basic economy): calculate the cost to cancel (note any fees or refunds) and book a new cheapest business ticket, then compute the net master card charge.
Compare both scenarios and identify which minimizes master card payment.
Proceed with the preferred option; if the agent confirms but policy restricts to one certificate per booking, propose splitting into three separate one-passenger reservations.

Assign payment per reservation: (a) self — $500 certificate + all gift card balances, master card covers remainder; (b) Aarav — certificate_9984806, master card covers remainder; (c) Evelyn — remaining certificate, master card covers remainder.
Sum the master card charges across all three bookings and report the final total owed on the master card.
